---
title: "figure1h"
author: "MJY"
date: "2024-09-11"
output: html_document
---

Figure 1h

################################################################################ 

# 

# OUTLINES:

# 1. Use bedtools to compute the number of H3K27ac peaks intersecting with each TAD region.

# 2. Separate TAD regions based on whether they overlap with H3K27ac peaks.

# 3. Use bedtools intersect to find the overlap between TAD regions with and without H3K27ac peaks.

# 4. Use deeptools to generate matrices of ChIP-seq signal values (H3K27ac) for regions of interest, including TAD regions with and without H3K27ac peaks.

# 5. Generates profile plots of ChIP-seq (H3K27ac) signals at regions of interest using plotProfile from deeptools.

# 

################################################################################ 

\#' \## <br> \#' 1. Use bedtools to compute the number of H3K27ac peaks intersecting with each TAD region.

```{bash eval=FALSE}

conda create -n bedtools
conda activate bedtools
conda install -c bioconda bedtools

cd /public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h
nohup bash 01.tad_h3k27ac_peak.sh 1>01.tad_h3k27ac_peak.log 2>&1 &

cd /public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/01.count
cat con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed | head
cat ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed | head

```

\#' “01.tad_h3k27ac_peak.sh” contains the following information

```{bash eval=FALSE}

#Usage:   bedtools intersect [OPTIONS] -a <bed/gff/vcf/bam> -b <bed/gff/vcf/bam> 
#                                      -c  For each entry in A, report the number of overlaps with B.

# Define the directory containing datum
TAD_18w=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/00.bed/tad
Peak_18w=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/00.bed/h3k27ac
output=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/01.count

mkdir -p ${output}

# Start the intersection for the control group
echo "bedtools intersect start!"
bedtools intersect -a ${TAD_18w}/con_18w_merged.TAD.bed \
				-b ${Peak_18w}/ctrl_18w_summits.bed \
				-c \
				> ${output}/con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed
echo "bedtools intersect finish!"

wait

# Start the intersection for the treated group
echo "bedtools intersect start!"
bedtools intersect -a ${TAD_18w}/ccl4_18w_merged.TAD.bed \
				-b ${Peak_18w}/ccl4_18w_summits.bed \
				-c \
				>${output}/ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed
echo "bedtools intersect finish!"

```

con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed

![](images/con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed.png){width="510"}

ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed

![](images/ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed.png){width="513"}

\#' \## <br> \#' 2. Separate TAD regions based on whether they overlap with H3K27ac peaks.

```{bash eval=FALSE}

cd ..
nohup bash 02.select.sh 1>02.select.log 2>&1 &

cd /public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/02.select
for file in *.bed
do
  echo "Displaying content of $file:"
  head "$file"
  echo "--------------------------"
done

```

\#' “02.select.sh” contains the following information

```{bash eval=FALSE}

# Define the directory containing data
input=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/01.count
output=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/02.select

mkdir -p ${output}

# Select TAD regions that have at least one overlapping H3K27ac peak for the control group and the treated group
awk '!$4 == 0' ${input}/con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed >  ${output}/con_18w_domain.bed
wait
awk '!$4 == 0' ${input}/ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed >  ${output}/ccl4_18w_domain.bed
echo "finish!"

# Select TAD regions that have zero overlapping H3K27ac peaks for the control group and the treated group
awk '$4 == 0' ${input}/con_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed >  ${output}/con_18w_zero_domain.bed
wait
awk '$4 == 0' ${input}/ccl4_18w_tad_peak_counts.bed >  ${output}/ccl4_18w_zero_domain.bed
echo "finish!"

```

con_18w_domain.bed & con_18w_zero_domain.bed

![](images/con_18w.bed.png){width="233"}

ccl4_18w_domain.bed & ccl4_18w_zero_domain.bed

![](images/ccl4_18w.bed.png){width="234"}

\#' \## <br> \#' 3. Use bedtools intersect to find the overlap between TAD regions with and without H3K27ac peaks.

```{bash eval=FALSE}

cd ..
nohup bash 03.overlap.sh 1>03.overlap.log 2>&1 &

cd /public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/03.overlap
cat h3k27ac_18w_overlap.bed | head
cat h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.bed | head

```

\#' “03.overlap.sh” contains the following information

```{bash eval=FALSE}

# Define the input where the input files are located
input=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/02.select
output=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/03.overlap

mkdir -p ${output}

# Use bedtools to find the intersection between TAD regions, either with or without H3K27ac peaks, in both the control group and the treated group.
bedtools intersect -a ${input}/con_18w_domain.bed -b ${input}/ccl4_18w_domain.bed > ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_overlap.bed

bedtools intersect -a ${input}/con_18w_zero_domain.bed -b ${input}/ccl4_18w_zero_domain.bed > ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.bed

```

h3k27ac_18w_overlap.bed

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_overlap.bed.png){width="501"}

h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.bed

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.bed-01.png){width="516"}

\#' \## <br> \#' 4. Use deeptools to generate matrices of ChIP-seq signal values (H3K27ac) for regions of interest, including TAD regions with and without H3K27ac peaks.

```{bash eval=FALSE}

cd ..
conda create -n deeptools
conda activate deeptools
conda install -c bioconda deeptools
nohup bash 04.compMat.sh 1>04.compMat.log 2>&1 &

cd /public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/04.martix
zcat h3k27ac_18w_overlap_matrix.gz | head
zcat h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap_matrix.gz | head

```

\#' “04.compMat.sh” contains the following information

```{bash eval=FALSE}

# usage: An example usage is:
#   computeMatrix reference-point -S <biwig file(s)> -R <bed file> -a 3000 -b 3000

# Define the paths for treated and control signal tracks, output directory, and input files
ccl4_18w=/public/home/mjy/HCC/chip/bam/h3k27ac_bam/merge/ccl4_18w/signalTrack
ctrl_18w=/public/home/mjy/HCC/chip/bam/h3k27ac_bam/merge/ctrl_18w/signalTrack
input=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/03.overlap
output=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/04.martix

mkdir -p ${output}

# Generate signal matrix for TAD regions with H3K27ac peak overlap (with overlap)
computeMatrix reference-point  --referencePoint center \
                               -p 24 \
                               -bs 100000 \
                               --skipZeros \
                               -a 2000000 -b 2000000 \
                               -R ${input}/h3k27ac_18w_overlap.bed \
                               -S ${ctrl_18w}/ctrl_18w_FE.sorted.bw ${ccl4_18w}/ccl4_18w_FE.sorted.bw  \
                               -o ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_overlap_matrix.gz 

wait
                              
# Generate signal matrix for TAD regions without H3K27ac peak overlap (zero overlap)
computeMatrix reference-point  --referencePoint center \
                               -p 24 \
                               -bs 100000 \
                               --skipZeros \
                               -a 2000000 -b 2000000 \
                               -R ${input}/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.bed \
                               -S ${ctrl_18w}/ctrl_18w_FE.sorted.bw ${ccl4_18w}/ccl4_18w_FE.sorted.bw  \
                               -o ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap_matrix.gz 
                               
```

h3k27ac_18w_overlap_matrix.gz

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap_matrix.gz.png)

h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap_matrix.gz

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_overlap_matrix.gz.png)

\#' \## <br> \#' 5. Generates profile plots of ChIP-seq (H3K27ac) signals at regions of interest using plotProfile from deeptools.

```{bash eval=FALSE}

cd ..
nohup bash 05.profile.sh 1>05.profile.log 2>&1 &

```

```{bash eval=FALSE}

# Define input and output directories
input=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/04.martix
output=/public/home/mjy/HCC/share/figure1h/05.profile

mkdir -p ${output}

# Plot the profile for TAD regions with H3K27ac peak overlap
plotProfile -m ${input}/h3k27ac_18w_overlap_matrix.gz \
            --outFileName ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_overlap.png \
            --yMin 0.6 \
            --yMax 1.0 \
            --plotHeight 8 \
            --plotWidth 8 \
            --colors "#00BFC4" "#F8766D" \
            --perGroup  \
            --startLabel "" \
            --endLabel "" \
            --samplesLabel  "Con_18w" "HCC_18w"    \
            --plotTitle "" \
            --refPointLabel "TAD center" \
            -T "h3k27ac_18w_overlap" 
wait
echo "h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap finish!"

# Plot the profile for TAD regions without H3K27ac peak overlap
plotProfile -m ${input}/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap_matrix.gz \
            --outFileName ${output}/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.png \
            --yMin 0.2 \
            --yMax 0.6 \
            --plotHeight 8 \
            --plotWidth 8 \
            --colors "#00BFC4" "#F8766D" \
            --perGroup  \
            --startLabel "" \
            --endLabel "" \
            --samplesLabel  "Con_18w" "HCC_18w"    \
            --plotTitle "" \
            --refPointLabel "TAD center" \
            -T "h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap" 
wait
echo "h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap finish!"


```

h3k27ac_18w_overlap.png

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_overlap.png){width="384"}

h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.png

![](images/h3k27ac_18w_zero_overlap.png){width="384"}
